body.title: Cognition module audit — classify 70+ modules as live/dead/experimental
body.result: audit_cognition_modules.py classifies 93 modules: LIVE=72, EXPERIMENTAL=17, PLACEHOLDER=0, ORPHAN=4 (observer, pipeline_manager, prefrontal_cortex, reasoning_cache — verify before deleting). Report at lab/claudecode/reports/cognition_module_audit_20260430-003029.md. Palace summary at theigors/audits/cognition_module_audit/latest. Per-module subsystem_index nodes deferred (balloons scope). 4193 regression pass.
body.description:
wild_igor/igor/cognition/ contains 84 .py modules. The palace subsystem_index names ~10 as load-bearing. The remaining ~70 modules have unknown status: live and wired in transitively, experimental dead-end, orphan, or moved-but-not-deleted. This is the single biggest 'is this built?' confusion source for fresh sessions — it broke Sonnet repeatedly last week.

Work: walk every cognition/*.py. For each, determine status (live / experimental / orphan / placeholder) by (a) checking importers via grep, (b) reading the top-of-file docstring, (c) checking call sites against main.py and turn_pipeline.py. Classify each. Update palace subsystem_index to reflect findings: add nodes for live-but-undocumented modules, mark stale placeholders, file removal candidates for orphans (don't auto-delete — surface for Akien review).

Affected files:
- wild_igor/igor/cognition/*.py (read-only — just classifying)
- clan.memory_palace path 'theigors/subsystem_index/*' (additions/updates)
- lab/docs/capability_map.md §6.1 (close out the open question)

Design rules:
- postgres-only: applicable — palace writes only.
- oop-first: not applicable — investigation work.
- docs-in-code: applies via top-of-file docstrings as canonical source.
- no-new-memory-schemas: applicable.
- test-plan-or-why-not: no automated test — output IS a doc + palace coverage delta.

Scope boundary:
IN: classification + palace updates for cognition/ tree.
OUT: actually deleting orphans (separate Akien-reviewed pass), refactoring live modules, brainstem/ (HIGH inertia — separate), memory/ (separate audit if needed).

Test plan:
- Output check: subsystem_index node count grows by N (where N = newly-classified live modules).
- Spot-check: pick 5 random cognition/ modules; for each, palace must answer 'is this live?' in one node lookup.
- No code-behavior test (this is a doc ticket).

Why now: surfaced by lab/docs/capability_map.md §6.1 (2026-04-27). The capability map exposed the gap; this ticket closes it.
